destinations.evidence: The reconstructed proof message includes block/error/input/output/precompile/previous-block/tx_hash fields and does not insert a success boolean before hashing and signature recovery.
The verifier delegates direct SPC verification to the shared signature reconstruction function, so the no-success payload is the direct-verification path.
destinations.snippet: let is_valid = verify_signature_core_impl(public_key, request.block_number, request.tx_hash, request.precompile_address, ...);

equivalence_argument: Direct SPC verification hashes the JSON generated by ritual-spc-signature::verify_signature_core. That function enumerates the Python-compatible fields and never inserts success, preserving the executor-signed payload shape.
notes: Mapped in the port through the shared ritual-spc-signature implementation used by ritual-spc-verification.
rationale: The commit title says the success field was unnecessary, and the diff removes ordered_map.insert("success", ...). This changes the exact signed payload that must match executor-generated proofs. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When reconstructing the JSON message for direct SPC signature verification, the verifier must not include a success boolean field; signatures are checked over the remaining Python-executor-compatible fields such as previous block metadata and tx_hash.
suggested_falsification_tests: Generate an SPC proof signed by the executor over a message without success. Verification must accept it. Then verify a signature generated over an otherwise identical message with an added success field and assert it does not verify against the no-success reconstruction.
summary: SPC direct signature reconstruction omits the success field.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Sign an SPC proof over the no-success JSON payload and assert verification succeeds; sign the same fields with an added success key and assert it fails against the current reconstruction.
title: SPC proof message excludes success field
